title: VibeIDE Shell Hardening Config
description: Workspace-level overrides for the run_command anti-misuse layer. Place at .vibe/shell-hardening.json — VibeIDE merges this with the bundled default ruleset.

allowedPatterns.description: Case-insensitive regex sources. If a shell command matches ANY of these, all default rules are bypassed (the command is allowed through). Use to whitelist patterns specific to this workspace.

disableDefaultRules.description: Stable ids of default rules to disable for this workspace. See shellHardeningDefaults.ts for the full list. Useful when a workspace genuinely prefers shell over the built-in tool (e.g. wants ripgrep CLI features).

extraRules.description: Additional workspace-specific rules, applied after the default ruleset. Use to block project-specific anti-patterns.

id.description: Stable identifier for telemetry and override-by-id.

bareName.description: Case-insensitive regex matched against the bare command name (no path, no .exe/.cmd/.bat/.ps1 suffix). Anchor with ^ and $ for an exact match.

tailMatches.description: Rule fires only if the tail-after-bareName matches this regex.

notPiped.description: Rule fires only if the command does NOT contain a pipe character.

exempts.description: Regex sources. If ANY matches the tail-after-bareName, the rule SKIPS even when requires would have fired.

suggestedTool.description: Name of the built-in VibeIDE tool the model should use instead.

hint.description: Hint shown to the model when this rule fires. {bareName} is substituted with the matched command name.
